function_v0.doc: Wire the settlement engine reference. One-shot; callable only by the admin bound\nat construction.
function_v0.name: initialize

function_v0.doc:
Bind the admin to the deploying identity atomically with deployment (HAL-01).

Runs in the same operation as `deploy`, eliminating the deploy\xe2\x86\x92configure window
an attacker could otherwise front-run.
function_v0.name: __constructor

function_v0.doc:
Refresh the TTL on the three persistent keys backing a single loan's collateral
escrow (`CollateralToken / CollateralAmount / CollateralReleased`). Long-lived
loans (term > the default Soroban persistent TTL window) need this to be called
on a schedule, otherwise the keys can be archived and the matching `claim_collateral`
/ `release_collateral` flow will revert with `CollateralNotFound` until a
`RestoreFootprint` is performed off-chain.

Admin-gated. The settlement engine already bumps these keys automatically on every
write (lock / migrate / release / claim), so this entrypoint is the explicit
operator handle for loans that have been *idle* for a long time. Errors with
`CollateralNotFound` if the loan was never recorded here.
function_v0.name: bump_loan_ttl

function_v0.doc: Execute a previously proposed upgrade once the timelock has elapsed.
function_v0.name: execute_upgrade

function_v0.doc: Stage an upgrade to be executed after the timelock period (48 h).
function_v0.name: propose_upgrade

function_v0.doc:
Refresh the TTL on the three persistent keys backing a single pre-match request's
pledged collateral (`RequestToken / RequestAmount / RequestReleased`). Same
rationale as `bump_loan_ttl` but for the pre-match escrow tracked under
`request_id` rather than `loan_id`.

Admin-gated. Errors with `RequestCollateralNotFound` if no such request escrow
exists (either never pledged or already migrated to a loan via `match_request`).
function_v0.name: bump_request_ttl

function_v0.doc: Lock collateral for an unfunded borrow request (`request_id`). Only settlement engine.
function_v0.name: lock_for_request

function_v0.doc: Release request escrow back to borrower (cancel). Only settlement engine.
function_v0.name: release_for_request

function_v0.doc: Move request escrow to `loan_id` storage (no token movement). Only settlement engine.
function_v0.name: migrate_request_to_loan

fields.doc: Address that supplied principal at match (EOA, lending vault, or other contract).
fields.name: lender

udt_union_v0.doc: SEP-040-style asset id for oracle `lastprice` (Stellar smart-contract / SAC token).
udt_union_v0.name: OracleAsset

fields.doc: Soft cap (in underlying smallest units). Equals `max_capacity` when the request had no\nsoft cap configured. `lending_vault::execute_match` allows borrower-only match when\n`min_capacity <= total_assets < max_capacity`.
fields.name: min_capacity

fields.doc: Loan-to-value at offer (basis points, e.g. 6000 = 60%). Used for buyout anchor when no oracle on loan.
fields.name: ltv_bps

fields.doc: Soft cap: smallest principal the borrower will accept at match. Equals `principal_amount`\nwhen no soft cap is set (full-fund-only behavior). Borrower-only match path activates\nwhen `min_principal_amount <= total_assets < principal_amount` (see `lending_vault::execute_match`).
fields.name: min_principal_amount

udt_struct_v0.doc: Bundled args for `lending_vault::initialize` \xe2\x80\x94 keeps the entrypoint under Soroban's 10-arg limit\nafter `min_capacity` was added. Constructed by `lending_vault_factory::deploy_vault`.
udt_struct_v0.name: VaultInitParams

udt_struct_v0.doc: Schedule step: at repayment, elapsed loan time selects the active tier via `from_seconds`.\n`rate_bps` is basis points of **principal** owed for that tier\xe2\x80\x94not APR/APY (no annualization).
udt_struct_v0.name: InterestRateTier

udt_struct_v0.doc: Packed oracle + liquidation price for `create_loan` (Soroban 10-arg limit).
udt_struct_v0.name: OracleLiquidation

cases.doc:
`create_loan` received an `OracleLiquidation` with a non-positive `price` (`<= 0`).
The stored `liquidation_price` is the threshold every `liquidate_by_price` comparison
keys off; a zero or negative value would make the loan either permanently liquidatable
or permanently unliquidatable, so reject it at creation instead of minting a loan with
a poisoned trigger.
cases.name: InvalidLiquidationPrice

cases.doc: `execute_vault_wasm_hash` called before the 48h upgrade timelock elapsed.
cases.name: VaultWasmHashTimelockActive

cases.doc: `share_name` / `share_symbol` length or emptiness (same rules as factory / offer book).
cases.name: InvalidShareMetadata

cases.doc: No pending upgrade has been proposed.
cases.name: NoPendingUpgrade

cases.doc: Upgrade timelock has not yet expired.
cases.name: UpgradeTimelockActive

cases.doc: `create_request` called while the borrower whitelist is enabled and the creator is not listed.
cases.name: BorrowerNotWhitelisted

cases.doc: `match_request` received a `principal_amount` outside the request's allowed band:\nEOA path requires equality with `request.principal_amount`; vault path requires\n`request.min_principal_amount <= principal_amount <= request.principal_amount`.
cases.name: InvalidPrincipalAmount

cases.doc: Borrow request creator cannot be the lender on their own request \xe2\x80\x94 symmetric to\n`LendingVaultError::BorrowerCannotDeposit`. Blocks both direct self-match and the\nzero-cost rug-pull where a borrower drains LP capital from their own vault.
cases.name: BorrowerCannotLend

cases.doc:
Whitelisted oracle returned a non-positive `price` (`<= 0`). All collateralization,
buyout, and liquidation math assumes positive prices; a negative or zero quote
would invert sign on collateral value or zero-divide later, so reject at the
boundary instead of trusting downstream math to catch it.
cases.name: OraclePriceInvalid

cases.doc:
`match_request` called at or after `created_at + funding_window_seconds`. The
funding window is the borrower's advertised matching cutoff; past it, the request
is unwindable via `expire_unfunded_offer` and must not be turned into a live loan.
Symmetric to `FundingWindowActive = 222` (returned by `expire_unfunded_offer`
before the deadline) \xe2\x80\x94 together they form a clean boundary at the deadline.
cases.name: FundingWindowExpired

cases.doc:
Spot `lastprice` deviates from the recent SEP-0040 `prices(asset, records)` average
by more than `MAX_ORACLE_DEVIATION_BPS`. A single spot quote is trivially wickable
(flash-loan/sandwich the feed for one ledger) and every liquidation/buyout decision
keys off it, so a lone outlier must not be allowed to trigger a seizure. Reject and
force the caller to retry once the feed re-converges to its own recent history.
cases.name: OraclePriceDeviation

cases.doc: Pulled amount from settlement engine did not match balance delta (fee-on-transfer or auth bug).
cases.name: DefaultCollateralTransferMismatch

cases.doc: Cannot sweep the vault contract's own share ledger address.
cases.name: CannotSweepShareToken

cases.doc: Borrow request creator cannot deposit into the vault for their own request.
cases.name: BorrowerCannotDeposit

cases.doc: `execute_match` called when `total_assets < min_capacity` \xe2\x80\x94 soft cap not yet reached.
cases.name: SoftCapNotReached

cases.doc: `deposit` / `mint` called while the offer book's lender whitelist is enabled and the depositor is not listed.
cases.name: LenderNotWhitelisted

cases.doc: Pro-rata payout floored to zero \xe2\x80\x94 redeeming would burn shares for nothing. The\nredeemer keeps their shares instead of silently losing them.
cases.name: RedemptionTooSmall

fields.doc: Soft cap; equals `max_capacity` when no soft cap was configured on the borrow request.
fields.name: min_capacity

fields.doc: Lending vault contract: OpenZeppelin `FungibleToken` + `Vault` share ledger and metadata (`name` / `symbol` / `decimals`) live at this address \xe2\x80\x94 use it as the **token contract id** for listings (CoinGecko, etc.), DEX pools, and wallets.
fields.name: vault

udt_struct_v0.doc: CollateralVault: borrower collateral escrowed against a live `loan_id` (`lock_collateral`).
udt_struct_v0.name: CollateralLockedEvent

udt_struct_v0.doc: CollateralVault: loan collateral seized to a recipient on default/liquidation\n(`claim_collateral`).
udt_struct_v0.name: CollateralClaimedEvent

udt_struct_v0.doc: CollateralVault: request escrow re-keyed from `request_id` to `loan_id` on match\n(`migrate_request_to_loan`); no tokens move.
udt_struct_v0.name: CollateralMigratedEvent

udt_struct_v0.doc: CollateralVault: loan collateral returned to a recipient on repayment/unwind\n(`release_collateral`).
udt_struct_v0.name: CollateralReleasedEvent

fields.doc: Soft cap (smallest principal accepted at match). Equals `principal_amount` when no\nsoft cap was configured at creation; can be lowered via `offer_book::set_min_principal_amount`.
fields.name: min_principal_amount

udt_struct_v0.doc: LendingVaultFactory: a proposed vault WASM hash was *executed* after the timelock and is\nnow the template every future `deploy_vault` stamps out.
udt_struct_v0.name: VaultWasmHashUpdatedEvent

udt_struct_v0.doc: LendingVaultFactory: a new vault WASM hash was *proposed* (HAL-10). Starts the\n`UPGRADE_TIMELOCK_SECONDS` notice window; `executable_after` is the earliest ledger\ntimestamp `execute_vault_wasm_hash` will accept.
udt_struct_v0.name: VaultWasmHashProposedEvent

udt_struct_v0.doc: LendingVaultFactory: a pending vault WASM hash proposal was *cancelled* before execution.
udt_struct_v0.name: VaultWasmHashCancelledEvent

udt_struct_v0.doc: CollateralVault: borrower collateral escrowed against a pre-match `request_id`\n(`lock_for_request`).
udt_struct_v0.name: RequestCollateralLockedEvent

udt_struct_v0.doc: CollateralVault: pre-match request escrow returned to a recipient (`release_for_request`).
udt_struct_v0.name: RequestCollateralReleasedEvent
